name: Link has non-empty accessible name
description: This rule checks that each link has a non-empty accessible name.
applicability_text: This rule applies to any [HTML element][] that is an [inheriting semantic][] `link` [included in the accessibility tree][].
expectations: Each target element has an [accessible name][] that is not empty (`""`).

examples.name: Passed Example 1
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its content.

examples.name: Passed Example 2
examples.description: This `div` element has an [explicit semantic role](#explicit-role) of `link` and an [accessible name][] from its content.
examples.code: Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI)

examples.name: Passed Example 3
examples.description: This `button` element has an [explicit semantic role](#explicit-role) of `link` and an [accessible name][] from its content.

examples.name: Passed Example 4
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] via `aria-label`

examples.name: Passed Example 5
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] via `title`.

examples.name: Passed Example 6
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its content via the `title` on the `img` element.

examples.name: Passed Example 7
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its content.

examples.name: Passed Example 8
examples.description: This `a` element has an [accessible name][] from its content via `aria-labelledby` on the `img` element.
examples.code: Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI)

examples.name: Passed Example 9
examples.description: This `a` element placed off screen has an [accessible name][] from its content.
examples.code: Web Accessibility Initiative (WAI)

examples.name: Passed Example 10
examples.description: This `area` element has a [semantic role][] of `link` and an [accessible name][] via `alt`.

examples.name: Passed Example 11
examples.description: This `a` element is an [inheriting semantic][] `link` given by its `role="doc-biblioref"` attribute as well as an [accessible name] given by its child text node.

examples.name: Failed Example 1
examples.description: This `a` element has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 2
examples.description: This `a` element with an image has an empty [accessible name][]. The image is decorative and is marked as such with an empty `alt` [attribute value][].

examples.name: Failed Example 3
examples.description: This `a` element with an image has an empty [accessible name][]. The image is decorative because it has a `role` [attribute value][] of `presentation`.

examples.name: Failed Example 4
examples.description: This `a` element with an image has an empty [accessible name][]. The image is decorative because it has a `role` [attribute value][] of `none`.

examples.name: Failed Example 5
examples.description: This `a` element with an `img` with an empty `title` has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 6
examples.description: This `a` element with an `img` with an `aria-labelledby` has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 7
examples.description: This `a` element with an `img` with an `aria-labelledby` referencing a non-existing id has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 8
examples.description: This `a` element placed off screen has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 9
examples.description: This `area` element has a [semantic role][] of `link` and an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 10
examples.description: This `a` element has an [explicit role][] of `none`. However, it is [focusable][] (by default). Thus it has a [semantic role][] of `link` due to [Presentational Roles Conflict Resolution][]. It has an empty [accessible name][].

examples.name: Failed Example 11
examples.description: This `a` element is an [inheriting semantic][] `link` given by its `role="doc-biblioref"` attribute but it has an empty [accessible name].

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 1
examples.description: This `a` element does not have a [semantic role][] of `link` because it has been changed to `button`.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 2
examples.description: This `a` element is not [included in the accessibility tree][] due to `display: none`.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 3
examples.description: This `a` element is not [included in the accessibility tree][] due to `visibility: hidden`.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 4
examples.description: This `a` element is not [included in the accessibility tree][] due to `aria-hidden="true"`.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 5
examples.description: This `area` element does not have the role of link because it does not have an `href` attribute.

examples.name: Inapplicable Example 6
examples.description: This `a` element does not have the role of link because it does not have an `href` attribute.

assumptions: The rule assumes that all links are [user interface components](https://www.w3.org/TR/WCAG22/#dfn-user-interface-components) as defined by WCAG 2. When the link role is used on elements that do not behave as links, failing this rule might not mean that the success criteria are failed.
accessibility_support:
- For `area` elements that have an `href` attribute, but are not nested inside a `map` element, there are differences between browsers and assistive technology on if the `area` is [included in the accessibility tree][].
- Implementation of [Presentational Roles Conflict Resolution][] varies from one browser or assistive technology to another. Depending on this, some [semantic][semantic role] `link` elements can fail this rule with some technology but users of other technologies would not experience any accessibility issue.
- Accessibility support for some elements inheriting the semantic role of `link` (e.g. elements with `doc-*` attributes) may vary depending on the assistive technology in use.
